Notes: Warm amber-tan, very even surface, no crawling. Excellent on throwing marks.
Deep umber-brown with subtle texture at heavier applications. Runs slightly if over 3mm.
Pale grey-green. Very food safe, good on bowls. Needs 2 coats minimum.
Dusty ochre-orange. More interesting than expected. Slight variation kiln to kiln.
Muted sage with slight blue undertone. Popular in class. Low cobalt keeps it subtle.
Rich teal-blue, glassy surface. Good for accent pieces. Use sparingly on matte clay.
Clean off-white matte. Very reliable. Good teaching glaze - fires predictably.
Deep terracotta-rust. Heavy iron plus small manganese. Variation adds character.
Cool neutral grey with subtle rutile texture. Works well with iron brown liner.
Warm beige-linen. Hits an earthy note without going brown. Good for dinnerware sets.
Deep navy blue. High cobalt. Food safe. Good on functional ware.
Soft butter yellow with slight warmth. Titanium gives opacity. Popular with students.
Dark grey with iron depth. Subtle. Good complement to lighter clay body.
Stone grey-tan with warm undertone. Very food safe result. Repeat for holiday stock.
Light copper-gold effect with rutile. Highly variable kiln to kiln. Test only pieces.
